terms.definition: Relating to dogs or members of the family Canidae.
terms.exampleSentence: The canine patient presented with acute vomiting and lethargy.
terms.contextNotes: Adjective and noun in clinical contexts.

terms.definition: Relating to cats or members of the family Felidae.
terms.exampleSentence: Feline patients often hide signs of pain until illness is advanced.
terms.contextNotes: Used in conditions like FLUTD and FIV.

terms.definition: Relating to horses or members of the family Equidae.
terms.exampleSentence: Equine colic remains a leading cause of emergency call-outs.
terms.contextNotes: Common in farm and stud practice.

terms.definition: Relating to cattle or members of the family Bovidae.
terms.exampleSentence: Bovine mastitis significantly reduces milk yield in dairy herds.
terms.contextNotes: Used widely in herd-health reporting.

terms.definition: Relating to sheep.
terms.exampleSentence: Ovine foot rot was diagnosed across two thirds of the flock.
terms.contextNotes: Common in mixed and farm animal practice.

terms.definition: Relating to pigs or members of the family Suidae.
terms.exampleSentence: The porcine herd was vaccinated against erysipelas last month.
terms.contextNotes: Standard term in pig medicine and biosecurity.

terms.definition: Relating to goats.
terms.exampleSentence: Caprine arthritis encephalitis is endemic in some dairy goat herds.
terms.contextNotes: Often discussed alongside ovine medicine.

terms.definition: A mammal of the order Lagomorpha, including rabbits and hares.
terms.exampleSentence: Lagomorph dental disease commonly results from low-fibre diets.
terms.contextNotes: Used in exotic and small-mammal practice.

terms.definition: Relating to birds.
terms.exampleSentence: The avian patient required oxygen supplementation during anaesthesia.
terms.contextNotes: Common in exotic, poultry and zoo practice.

terms.definition: Relating to reptiles, including snakes, lizards, turtles and crocodilians.
terms.exampleSentence: Reptilian patients require species-specific temperature gradients during recovery.
terms.contextNotes: Husbandry advice is often part of OET letters.

terms.definition: A non-traditional companion species such as reptiles, birds or small mammals.
terms.exampleSentence: The clinic referred the exotic patient to a specialist for advanced imaging.
terms.contextNotes: Includes lagomorphs, ferrets, parrots and reptiles.

terms.definition: The first and largest compartment of a ruminant's stomach where microbial fermentation occurs.
terms.exampleSentence: Rumen pH had dropped, suggesting subacute ruminal acidosis in the herd.
terms.contextNotes: Central to ruminant nutrition and metabolic disease.

terms.definition: The fourth and 'true' glandular stomach of a ruminant.
terms.exampleSentence: A left-displaced abomasum was confirmed on auscultation and percussion.
terms.contextNotes: LDA is a common dairy cow presentation.

terms.definition: The ridge between the shoulder blades of a horse, used as a reference for height.
terms.exampleSentence: The pony measured 13.2 hands at the withers.
terms.contextNotes: Standard reference point in equine examination.

terms.definition: The joint between the cannon bone and pastern in horses and other ungulates.
terms.exampleSentence: Marked swelling of the fetlock was noted on the near forelimb.
terms.contextNotes: Common site of equine lameness.

terms.definition: The tarsal joint of the hind limb in quadrupeds, equivalent to the human ankle.
terms.exampleSentence: Mild effusion of the hock was palpable bilaterally.
terms.contextNotes: Common site of bog spavin and bone spavin in horses.

terms.definition: A vestigial digit on the medial aspect of a dog's or cat's lower limb.
terms.exampleSentence: The torn dewclaw was trimmed and bandaged under sedation.
terms.contextNotes: Often injured in active or working dogs.

terms.definition: The common opening for the digestive, urinary and reproductive tracts in birds and reptiles.
terms.exampleSentence: A cloacal swab was submitted for Salmonella culture.
terms.contextNotes: Key landmark in avian and reptile examinations.

terms.definition: An expandable pouch in the oesophagus of birds used for food storage.
terms.exampleSentence: The crop was full and doughy on palpation, suggesting stasis.
terms.contextNotes: Crop stasis is common in psittacine paediatrics.
commonMistakes: ignoring it in mammals (it does not exist)

terms.definition: The muscular stomach of a bird that grinds food, often with the aid of grit.
terms.exampleSentence: Radiographs showed multiple metallic foreign bodies within the gizzard.
terms.contextNotes: Heavy-metal toxicosis is a key differential.

terms.definition: The nictitating membrane, a translucent inner eyelid that protects and moistens the eye.
terms.exampleSentence: Protrusion of the third eyelid was noted in both eyes.
terms.contextNotes: Cherry eye refers to gland prolapse of the third eyelid.

terms.definition: The horny keratinised covering of the distal limb in ungulates.
terms.exampleSentence: The hoof wall was overgrown and required corrective trimming.
terms.contextNotes: Plural is 'hooves' or 'hoofs'.

terms.definition: The triangular elastic structure on the underside of the equine hoof that aids shock absorption.
terms.exampleSentence: The frog was pared back to expose the abscess tract.
terms.contextNotes: Important landmark when locating hoof abscesses.

terms.definition: Paired sacs adjacent to the anus that secrete a scent-marking fluid.
terms.exampleSentence: Manual expression of the anal glands resolved the perineal discomfort.
terms.contextNotes: Frequent presenting complaint in small animal practice.

terms.definition: The visible external part of the ear that collects sound.
terms.exampleSentence: An aural haematoma was developing on the left ear pinna.
terms.contextNotes: Aural haematoma is a frequent canine presentation.

terms.definition: The prominence of the greater tubercle of the humerus, used as an anatomical landmark.
terms.exampleSentence: The injection was administered just caudal to the point of shoulder.
terms.contextNotes: Used in equine and large-animal examination.

terms.definition: The femorotibial joint of the hind limb, equivalent to the human knee.
terms.exampleSentence: Drawer sign was positive in the right stifle, suggesting cruciate disease.
collocations: cranial cruciate ligament of the stifle
terms.contextNotes: Cruciate rupture is a leading orthopaedic case.

terms.definition: The part of an equine limb between the fetlock and the hoof.
terms.exampleSentence: Mud fever lesions were evident along the pastern.
terms.contextNotes: Common site for equine pastern dermatitis ('mud fever').

terms.definition: Vertical distance from the ground to the highest point of the withers.
terms.exampleSentence: The foal's withers height had increased by 4 cm since the last visit.
terms.contextNotes: Standard biometric for horses and ponies.

terms.definition: One of the two horn-covered digits of a cloven-hoofed animal such as cattle.
terms.exampleSentence: A sole ulcer was identified on the lateral claw of the right hind.
terms.contextNotes: Cattle have two claws per foot; lameness scoring is per claw.
